scope: local Eliza-1 voice/text streaming latency path

changes.result: OpenAI-compatible chat completions now use streaming when an onTextChunk callback is supplied, forwarding accepted text deltas before full response completion.
LocalInferenceEngine forwards streamed text chunks into the active voice scheduler while generation is in flight, with full-text fallback for non-streaming backends.
VoiceScheduler and EngineVoiceBridge now support explicit phrase prewarming so common assistant phrases can be synthesized once and served from cache.
Default voice phrase cap is reduced to 8 tokens and max in-flight TTS phrases can be constrained with ELIZA_VOICE_MAX_IN_FLIGHT_PHRASES.
Root Vitest excludes embedded .git/.claude worktrees, aliases workspace packages to source, and uses app-core-compatible timeouts.

remainingRuntimeWork: Expose true DFlash accept/reject token events from the fused native runtime or llama-server stream; current HTTP streaming path forwards final accepted text deltas and cannot cancel TTS for speculative branches the server never emitted.
Make the product chat/voice UI call the streamed local path directly instead of waiting for post-hoc TTS on completed assistant messages.
Record first-token, first-audio, peak RSS, and thermal metrics from the iOS/Android app shells with the same streaming/prewarm settings.
Backport the dflash-draft architecture loader into the optimized milady fused runtime so the publishable binary does not rely on the nonpublishable DFlash loader bridge.
